---
title: "Configuration"
---

All settings live under the `nowPlaying.*` namespace in VSCode's settings.

| Setting                       | Default                          | Description |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------|-------------|
| `nowPlaying.format`           | `$(unmute) {artist} - {title}`   | Status bar format. Placeholders: `{artist}`, `{title}`, `{album}`, `{player}`, `{status}`. Supports `$(icon)` syntax. |
| `nowPlaying.maxLength`        | `50`                             | Max characters before truncation with ellipsis. |
| `nowPlaying.alignment`        | `right`                          | Status bar alignment (`left` or `right`). |
| `nowPlaying.priority`         | `100`                            | Status bar priority (higher = further from edges). |
| `nowPlaying.showControls`     | `true`                           | Show separate prev/next status bar buttons. |
| `nowPlaying.preferredPlayer`  | `""`                             | Preferred MPRIS player suffix (e.g. `spotify`). Empty = first available. |
| `nowPlaying.sidecarPath`      | `""`                             | Path to the `now-playing` binary. Empty = use bundled binary. |
| `nowPlaying.formatRules`      | `[]`                             | Ordered overrides. Each rule is `{ when: { field: regex, ... }, format: "..." }`. First match wins. |

## Per-media overrides

`nowPlaying.formatRules` lets you swap the template for specific media. Each
rule's `when` block is a field-to-regex map (case-insensitive); all listed
fields must match. Matchable fields: `title`, `artist`, `album`, `player`,
`status`.

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.formatRules": [
    { "when": { "title": "Netflix" }, "format": "$(device-camera-video) Netflix" },
    { "when": { "player": "spotify" }, "format": "$(music) {artist} - {title}" }
  ]
}
```

## Format placeholders

The `nowPlaying.format` string is rendered with these substitutions:

| Placeholder  | Value                                |
|--------------|--------------------------------------|
| `{artist}`   | Track artist                         |
| `{title}`    | Track title                          |
| `{album}`    | Album name                           |
| `{player}`   | MPRIS player identifier              |
| `{status}`   | `Playing`, `Paused`, or `Stopped`    |

You can also embed VSCode codicons via the `$(icon-name)` syntax:

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.format": "$(unmute) {artist} - {title}",
  "nowPlaying.maxLength": 60
}
```

## Picking a specific player

If multiple MPRIS players are running, set the preferred one by the suffix of
its bus name (the part after `org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.`):

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.preferredPlayer": "spotify"
}
```

List currently exposed players:

```bash
busctl --user list | grep mpris
```
